key_facts: retention_in_days = 14 is already set — resource is compliant
terraform_action must be NONE — do NOT generate any Terraform for this finding
ingestion volume is low (0.25 GB/month) — S3 archival not warranted
368 untagged images accumulating — no lifecycle policy set
61.7 GB total; lifecycle reduces cost from $6.17 to ~$0.44/month
aws_ecr_lifecycle_policy must be a separate resource (not inline)
rules: expire untagged images after 1 day, cap tagged images at 30
data_loss_acknowledged = true — existing untagged images will be deleted

decision_summary.rationale: The log group already has a retention period of 14 days, which is compliant and no action is needed.

/aws/lambda/user-auth.technical_explanation: The log group /aws/lambda/user-auth has a retention period of 14 days, which is already compliant with the best practices. No action is required.

decision_summary.rationale: Adding a lifecycle policy to the ECR repository will help manage untagged images and save costs.

backend-service-ecr.technical_explanation: The ECR repository backend-service has 368 untagged images, which can be managed by adding a lifecycle policy. This will help in reducing the storage costs and maintaining a clean repository.

risks: Potential data loss if untagged images are deleted
risk_assessment.verification_steps: Verify that the lifecycle policy does not delete any critical images. Ensure that the tagged images are not affected by the policy.

llm_response.group_summary: The log group /aws/lambda/user-auth is already compliant with the retention policy. The ECR repository backend-service can benefit from a lifecycle policy to manage untagged images and save costs. Total savings of $5.73 per month can be achieved by implementing the lifecycle policy.
